pattern: A sliding window over the sorted fruit positions, where the 'cost' of a window is the shortest walk from the start that still visits both its ends -- go one way then double back, so the cost is the total span plus the smaller of the two one-directional detours.
hints: Because positions are already sorted, think of the answer as choosing a contiguous window [left, right] of fruits and asking whether it is reachable within k steps.

Reaching a window that spans both sides of startPos costs (distance to the left end) + (distance to the right end) + min(those two distances) -- you commit to one direction first, walk back through the overlap, then finish the other side.
Slide a two-pointer window over the sorted positions: grow the right end, and shrink the left end only while the window's cost exceeds k, tracking the best prefix-sum total along the way.

edge_inputs.description: single fruit exactly at the start, free to collect
single fruit unreachable with zero steps
fruits on both sides of start, must pick a direction and double back
just barely enough steps to reach the farthest fruit one-directionally
k is large enough to collect every fruit
start position sits before every fruit
start position sits after every fruit
start and fruit positions at the extreme constraint values

stress.description: random strictly increasing positions across the full position range with random start and budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    positions = sorted(rng.sample(range(0, 200001), n))
    fruits = [[p, rng.randint(1, 10000)] for p in positions]
    start_pos = rng.randint(0, 200000)
    k = rng.randint(0, 200000)
    return [fruits, start_pos, k]
